title: Nursery Rhyme. CCCXLV. Games.
content:
Here we come a piping,
        First in spring, and then in May;
        The queen she sits upon the sand,
        Fair as a lily, white as a wand:
        King John has sent you letters three,
        And begs you'll read them unto me. - 
        We can't read one without them all,
        So pray, Miss Bridget, deliver the ball!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Unknown, titled "Nursery Rhyme. CCCXLV. Games.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
